classifications.phrase: decided to increase the cash rate target by 25 basis points
Inflation picked up materially in the second half of 2025
sharply higher fuel and related commodity prices, which are already adding to inflation
Short-term measures of inflation expectations have also risen
underlying inflation peaking higher than was expected in February
demand growth slows and capacity pressures ease in response to higher interest rates
plausible scenarios where inflation is higher and activity lower than envisaged
further upward pressure on global energy prices
growth to be lower in Australias major trading partners and also in Australia
Higher fuel prices are adding to inflation
second-round effects on prices for goods and services more broadly
high inflation recorded around the start of 2026, reflecting capacity pressures
inflation is likely to remain above target for some time
risks remain tilted to the upside, including to inflation expectations
appropriate to increase the cash rate target

payload.rationale: The RBA statement signals a hawkish stance dominated by inflation concerns, with multiple references to elevated and rising inflation pressures, capacity constraints, and upside risks to inflation expectations, justifying the 25 basis point rate increase.
